{"query":"cabinet ministers resigned Keir Starmer June 2026 list names","model":"mako","response_time_ms":814,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.instituteforgovernment.org.uk/explainer/ministers-resigning-government","title":"Ministers resigning from government | Institute for Government","content":"In 2021, Johnson sacked the minister for veterans Johnny Mercer after he threatened to resign over lack of legal protections for British troops who fought in Northern Ireland in the Overseas Operations Bill.
After a minister resigns, the prime minister also has to consider who to appoint to fill the new vacancy.
An IfG expert webinar explored the fall-out from the vetting and appointment row and the sacking of Sir Olly Robbins.
Former No.10 director of communications Steph Driver joins the podcast line-up on a huge week in British politics."},{"url":"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_Labour_Party_leadership_crisis","title":"2026 Labour Party leadership crisis - Wikipedia","content":"Following Burnham's victory in Makerfield and calls for an immediate leadership challenge by Labour MPs, Starmer announced his resignation on 22 June 2026.
After all other reported candidates declined to stand, Burnham received nominations from more than 90% of Labour MPs and a sufficient number of party affiliates by 15 July, making it mathematically impossible for another candidate to stand.
In December 2024, the government announced prominent New Labour figure Peter Mandelson's appointment as the British ambassador to the United States to strengthen their relationship with the incoming Trump administration.
Foreign Secretary Yvette Cooper dismissed the most senior civil servant in the Foreign Office, Olly Robbins.
On 11 June 2026, Secretary of State for Defence John Healey resigned from the cabinet, citing disagreements with the government's defence spending plans.
According to LabourList, on 14 May 2026 158 Labour MPs had shown support for Starmer, 103 had called for him to resign or set out a timetable, and 143 were either unknown or had not taken a position.
That number expanded to 160 a couple of days later.
However, there were some allegations by some MPs that they had their names put on the list without their permission.
Archived from the original on 22 April 2026.
Archived from the original on 19 June 2026."},{"url":"https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2026/6/22/uks-starmer-announces-resignation-from-office-after-mounting-pressure","title":"UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer announces resignation | Politics News | Al Jazeera","content":"The announcement paves the way for a contest among Labour MPs to become Britain’s seventh prime minister in just more than a decade, with Starmer’s main rival Andy Burnham expected to be the frontrunner.
In 2024, Starmer won the biggest parliamentary majority for Labour since 1997, after 14 years of ‌Conservative-led government."},{"url":"https://www.tutor2u.net/politics/blog/ministerial-resignations","title":"Ministerial Resignations | Blog | Politics | tutor2u","content":"In the period between the July 2024 general election and early March 2026, 12 ministers resigned from Keir Starmer’s Labour government, an unusually high number for an administration which has been in power for just over 18 months.
Under the convention of collective responsibility, ministers can debate and disagree on policy issues behind closed doors but as soon as the government has decided its stance on any matter, ministers are expected to fall in line and defend the official position in parliament and in public.
In February 2025, Anneliese Dodds, then Minister for Development, resigned from her post because she ‘fundamentally disagreed’ with the Starmer government’s proposed cuts to the international aid budget."},{"url":"https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/c0veg88g7jyo","title":"Who's in Keir Starmer's cabinet?","content":"Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer has announced his resignation, nearly two years after leading the Labour Party into power in July 2024."}]}
